name: Double-Glazed Chocolate Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
Heat oven to 350°F; grease a 10-cup fluted tube pan, preferably Teflon-coated. In a large bowl combine flour, sugar, cocoa powder, grated orange peel, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, oil, eggs, and orange juice. Beat just until blended with an electric mixer on low speed. Increase speed to medium and beat 2 minutes until well combined and smooth. Stir in shredded carrots, raisins, and chopped walnuts. Pour batter into prepared pan.
Bake for 45-50 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool cake in pan on a wire rack for 15 minutes; remove from pan to cool completely on a rack. Meanwhile, in a medium bowl mix cream cheese, powdered sugar, 2 tablespoons milk, and vanilla extract with an electric mixer on medium speed until blended and smooth. Remove 1 tablespoon of this mixture to a small bowl.
To the remaining mixture in the medium bowl, add cocoa powder and the remaining 1-2 tablespoons milk; beat until smooth and at proper consistency for glazing. Spoon chocolate glaze over the top of the cooled cake so it drips randomly down the sides. Drizzle plain glaze over the chocolate to serve.
